title: Code Intelligence Engine — Vector Search, Knowledge Graph & Semantic Retrieval
overview: Upgrade the AI Code Knowledge System from heuristic/substring search to a multi-layered intelligence engine combining hybrid BM25+vector retrieval, a heterogeneous knowledge graph, cross-cutting feature summaries, and agentic query routing. This is driven by the need to scale from ~100 files to 3000-4000 file codebases where current substring matching and token-based search produce too many false positives and miss semantic relationships. The system adds four new storage layers under .knowledge/ (vectors, graph, features, BM25 index) and exposes three new MCP tools (semantic_search, explore_graph, get_feature_context) while enhancing existing tools with vector reranking and graph-aware context.

motivation.problem: At 3000+ files, the current system's substring-based find_symbol returns hundreds of noisy matches, find_template_file's token matching produces excessive false positives, and agents cannot efficiently answer cross-cutting questions like 'how does authentication work across the codebase'. The system lacks semantic understanding — it can find exact names but cannot discover conceptually related code.
research_backing: CodeSage (2023-2024): Code-specific embeddings outperform OpenAI text-embedding-3-large by 34% on code-to-code search
CodeXEmbed (COLM 2025): Multilingual code embeddings, 20%+ over previous SOTA on CoIR benchmark
Prometheus (July 2025): Unified FileNode/ASTNode/TextNode knowledge graph with working memory for multi-hop code navigation
LocAgent (ACL 2025): Heterogeneous graph + LLM multi-hop reasoning for code localization
Code Graph Model (NeurIPS 2025): Graph-structured attention achieving 43% on SWE-bench Lite
Hybrid BM25+Vector retrieval with RRF: 15-30% recall improvement over single-method retrieval (consensus from 2024-2025 IR research)
RepoSummary (2025): Feature-oriented cross-cutting summarization outperforms file-level summaries for 'how does X work' queries
Agentic RAG Survey (Jan 2025): Query routing + retrieval self-evaluation patterns for autonomous agent retrieval

scale_impact.93_files: Current system works well — substring search is manageable, agent can guess file locations
scale_impact.500_files: Performance degrades — find_symbol noise increases, get_project_overview gets truncated
scale_impact.3000_files: System breaks down — agent cannot narrow search space, feature-level questions unanswerable, tool call counts explode

layers.name: Layer 1: Hybrid Search Engine
layers.purpose: Combine keyword precision (BM25) with semantic understanding (vector embeddings) for 15-30% better recall than either alone
components: EmbeddingGenerator — produces CodeSage embeddings during build-knowledge
HybridSearchEngine — LanceDB native hybrid search (BM25 + ANN vector)
ReciprocalRankFusion — merges BM25 and vector result lists (k=60)
ColBERTReranker — precision reranking on top-K candidates

embedding_strategy.model_primary: CodeSage-Base (768 dims, +34-41% over OpenAI embeddings on code-to-code search, available on HuggingFace)
embedding_strategy.implementation: SentenceTransformers library with model='codesage/codesage-base'. Handles batching, normalization, and EOS token automatically.
model_alternatives: CodeSage-Large (1024 dims, +34-41% quality, max performance when speed not critical)
CodeSage-Small (512 dims, lighter, faster for resource-constrained environments)
CodeXEmbed-1B (768 dims, open source alternative, +20% vs previous SOTA)
Ollama nomic-embed-text (local, free, privacy-preserving, aligns with existing Ollama summarizer)

codesage_setup.code_example: from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer; model = SentenceTransformer('codesage/codesage-base', trust_remote_code=True); embeddings = model.encode(texts, batch_size=32, show_progress_bar=True)

layers.name: Layer 2: Heterogeneous Knowledge Graph
layers.purpose: Unify AST nodes, file metadata, module structure, and feature mappings into a single traversable graph enabling multi-hop queries impossible with flat indexes

edge_types.source: computed lazily from vector proximity, cached

query_capabilities: Multi-hop traversal: 'find all callers of functions that import module X' (calls + imports, depth 3)
Reverse reachability: 'what features does this file participate in?' (reverse implements edge)
Neighborhood expansion: 'show me everything 2 hops from AuthService' (any edge type, configurable depth)
Typed path queries: 'files that contain symbols that call PaymentService.charge' (contains + calls)
Impact radius: 'what modules are affected if I change this file?' (imports + depends_on, transitive)

layers.name: Layer 3: Feature-Oriented Cross-Cutting Summaries
layers.purpose: Pre-compute feature-level understanding that spans multiple modules, enabling agents to answer 'how does X work' without reading 40 modules

discovery_method.step_1: Cluster file embeddings using HDBSCAN (density-based, no need to predefine K)
discovery_method.step_2: For each cluster, extract member file summaries + key symbols
discovery_method.step_3: LLM generates feature name, description, entry points, data flow, key symbols
discovery_method.step_4: Create feature → file edges in knowledge graph

feature_summary_schema.name: string — e.g., 'authentication', 'order-processing', 'payment-integration'
feature_summary_schema.description: string — 2-3 sentence overview of what this feature does
feature_summary_schema.involvedFiles: string[] — all files that participate in this feature
feature_summary_schema.entryPoints: string[] — where the feature flow starts (API endpoints, event handlers)
feature_summary_schema.dataFlow: string — how data moves through the feature end-to-end
feature_summary_schema.keySymbols: string[] — most important functions/classes for this feature
feature_summary_schema.relatedFeatures: string[] — other features that interact with this one

layers.update_strategy: Full recomputation on full rebuild only. Incremental builds skip feature discovery (too expensive for single-file changes). Features are regenerated when >10% of files change or on explicit --rebuild-features flag.

layers.name: Layer 4: Query Intelligence (Router + Reranker + Working Memory)
layers.purpose: Automatically classify queries, select optimal retrieval strategy, rerank results, and maintain session context across tool calls

components.name: QueryRouter
components.purpose: Classify incoming query into strategy without requiring the agent to choose

components.name: ColBERTReranker
components.purpose: Precision-optimize the top-K results from hybrid retrieval using late-interaction cross-encoding
components.when_applied: When hybrid search returns >10 candidates, rerank top 50 → return top K

components.name: WorkingMemory
components.purpose: Session-scoped cache that retains previously explored context across sequential MCP tool calls within a single agent session
behavior: Each MCP tool call checks working memory for relevant prior results before executing
Results are cached with 5-minute TTL and 50-entry LRU eviction
Agent can reference previously fetched context without re-querying
Session auto-expires after 10 minutes of inactivity
components.inspired_by: Prometheus (2025) working memory pattern for multi-step code navigation

storage_layout.description: All new storage lives under .knowledge/ — zero external infrastructure
tree: │   ├── nodes.json                # all node types with metadata
│   └── edges.json                # all edge types with weights

new_mcp_tools.name: semantic_search
new_mcp_tools.description: Natural language search across the entire codebase using hybrid BM25 + vector retrieval with reranking. Use when the agent needs to find code by description rather than exact name.

scope.description: Limit search to specific entity type

new_mcp_tools.output: Ranked list of files/symbols/features with relevance scores, snippets, and metadata
retrieval_pipeline: 1. QueryRouter classifies query → selects BM25/vector/hybrid strategy
2. LanceDB hybrid search returns topK * 3 candidates (over-fetch for reranking)
3. Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) merges BM25 and vector result lists
4. ColBERT reranker refines top 50 → returns top K
5. Results enriched with file summary metadata from summaries/cache.json
6. Results cached in working memory for subsequent tool calls
example_queries: error handling patterns in API routes

new_mcp_tools.name: explore_graph
new_mcp_tools.description: Traverse the code knowledge graph to find all code connected to a starting point via specific relationship types. Enables multi-hop queries impossible with flat indexes.
new_mcp_tools.output: Graph substructure: nodes visited (with metadata), edges traversed, depth at which each node was found
example_queries: { start: 'symbol:AuthService.login', edgeTypes: ['calls'], maxDepth: 3, direction: 'outgoing' } → full call tree from login
{ start: 'file:src/models/user.ts', edgeTypes: ['imports'], direction: 'incoming' } → all files that import user model
{ start: 'module:payments', edgeTypes: ['depends_on', 'contains'], maxDepth: 2 } → payment module's full dependency neighborhood
{ start: 'feature:authentication', edgeTypes: ['implements'], direction: 'incoming' } → all files participating in auth feature

new_mcp_tools.name: get_feature_context
new_mcp_tools.description: Understand how a feature or capability works across the entire codebase. Returns cross-cutting summaries spanning multiple modules with data flow, entry points, and key symbols.

query.description: Feature name or natural language description of the capability

topK.description: Number of matching features to return

new_mcp_tools.output: Feature summaries with involved files, entry points, data flow description, key symbols, and related features
retrieval_pipeline: 1. Vector search over features.lance table using query embedding
2. Top-K features retrieved with similarity scores
3. Each feature enriched with graph context (involved files, symbol details)
4. Data flow narrative included from feature cache
5. Related features linked via shared files
example_queries: how orders are processed end to end

enhanced_existing_tools.enhancement: When substring matching returns >20 results, apply vector reranking using the query as embedding input against symbols.lance. Reduces noise from O(hundreds) to top-10 most semantically relevant matches.
enhanced_existing_tools.fallback: If vector index unavailable, falls back to existing substring + Levenshtein ranking

enhanced_existing_tools.enhancement: Replace token-matching with vector similarity search over files.lance. Dramatically improves accuracy for natural language template descriptions.
enhanced_existing_tools.fallback: If vector index unavailable, falls back to existing token matching

enhanced_existing_tools.enhancement: Add a 'key features' section powered by feature summaries, giving agents an immediate understanding of what the project does, not just its structure.
enhanced_existing_tools.fallback: Section omitted if features not yet generated

enhanced_existing_tools.enhancement: Add 'similar files' section using vector proximity from files.lance. Helps agents discover related code when modifying a file.
enhanced_existing_tools.fallback: Section omitted if vector index unavailable

existing_phases_unchanged: Phase 1: Symbol Extraction (ts-morph AST walk → symbols.json)

new_phases.trigger: After Phase 4 (requires summaries) and Phase 1 (requires symbols)
new_phases.incremental_strategy: Only re-embed files whose contentHash changed (same hash mechanism as summary cache). LanceDB supports upsert by ID.
new_phases.cost: ~$0.02 per 4000 files with text-embedding-3-small, or free with local Ollama embeddings
new_phases.duration: ~30 seconds for 4000 files (batched API calls or local model)

new_phases.incremental_strategy: Rebuild affected subgraph only — remove nodes/edges for changed files, re-insert updated ones. Module and feature nodes only rebuilt on full rebuild.
Full rebuild only. Triggered when >10% of files changed or --rebuild-features flag. Too expensive for single-file incremental (requires reclustering + LLM calls).
new_phases.cost: ~$0.10-0.50 per full rebuild (LLM calls for ~20-50 feature summaries)
new_phases.duration: ~2-5 minutes for 4000 files (dominated by LLM summarization)

new_flags: --skip-vectors: Skip embedding generation (for environments without embedding API access)
--skip-features: Skip feature discovery (save cost on incremental builds)
--rebuild-features: Force feature rediscovery even if <10% files changed
--embedding-model <name>: Override embedding model (codesage | codexembed | ollama | openai)
env_vars: EMBEDDING_API_KEY: API key for cloud embedding models (not needed for Ollama)
EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: Batch size for embedding API calls (default: 100)

new_runtime.purpose: Embedded vector database with native hybrid search (BM25 + ANN). Zero-server, stores data as Lance files.

new_runtime.note: May be combined with above depending on package structure at implementation time

new_dev.purpose: Density-based clustering for feature discovery. Only used during build, not at query time.
new_dev.alternative: Could use simple k-means from ml-kmeans if HDBSCAN is too heavy

optional.purpose: Run CodeSage/CodeXEmbed locally via ONNX runtime. Avoids API dependency for embeddings.
Cohere Rerank API as alternative to local ColBERT. Higher quality but requires API key.
optional.note: Optional — system works without reranking, just with slightly lower precision

cloud_options.cost: Free via HuggingFace Inference API or self-hosted

local_options.note: Aligns with existing Ollama summarizer infrastructure

embedding_model_options.recommendation: Default to Ollama nomic-embed-text for zero-cost local operation. Use CodeSage-Large for maximum quality when API access is available.

memory_usage.vector_index: Loaded lazily via LanceDB (memory-mapped, not fully loaded)
memory_usage.graph: Loaded into memory on first query (~3MB for 3000 files), cached with existing 30-second invalidation

deliverables: scripts/lib/embedding-generator.ts — generates embeddings from summaries + symbols
Update to find-symbol.ts — vector reranking fallback when >20 results
acceptance_criteria: semantic_search('authentication middleware') returns relevant auth-related files ranked by relevance
find_symbol with >20 substring matches reranks using vectors and returns more relevant top-10
Embedding generation runs incrementally (only changed files re-embedded)
System works without embeddings (graceful fallback to existing behavior)
npm run build-knowledge --skip-vectors works without error

deliverables: scripts/lib/graph-builder.ts — builds heterogeneous graph from existing artifacts
mcp-server/tools/lib/graph-query.ts — multi-hop BFS with edge type filtering
acceptance_criteria: explore_graph('symbol:X', ['calls'], 2) returns the same results as find_callers for depth-2 (backward compatible)
explore_graph('file:X', ['imports'], 'incoming') returns all files that import X
Graph builds in <5 seconds for 3000-file project
Multi-hop queries like 'all callers of functions in module X' work correctly
Graph rebuilds incrementally for changed files

deliverables: scripts/lib/feature-extractor.ts — HDBSCAN clustering + LLM feature naming
Feature nodes + implements edges added to knowledge graph
acceptance_criteria: Feature discovery identifies meaningful feature clusters (not random groupings)
get_feature_context('authentication') returns involved files, entry points, and data flow
Features are linked to files via graph edges
Feature summaries are searchable via semantic_search
get_project_overview includes top features section

deliverables: mcp-server/tools/lib/query-router.ts — classifies queries into retrieval strategies
acceptance_criteria: Query router correctly classifies exact symbol names vs natural language vs graph queries
Working memory prevents redundant re-fetching within a 5-minute session window
Reranker improves precision when available, system works without it
get_implementation_context shows top-3 similar files when vector index exists
get_project_overview shows discovered features when available

updated_tool_order.description: Updated preferred tool order for CLAUDE.md after all phases are complete
order: 1. get_project_overview (first call — now includes key features)
5. get_implementation_context (understand a file before modifying — now includes similar files)
7. get_batch_summaries (when you know which files you need)
8. find_symbol (exact symbol lookup — now with vector reranking)

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Embedding model dependency adds infrastructure complexity
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Default to local Ollama embeddings (already supported). Cloud embeddings are optional. System gracefully degrades to existing behavior when vectors unavailable.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: LanceDB native binary compatibility across platforms
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: LanceDB publishes pre-built binaries for macOS, Linux, Windows. Falls back to pure-JS Vectra if native build fails.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Feature discovery produces low-quality clusters on some codebases
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Feature summaries are LLM-validated (bad clusters produce incoherent summaries that can be filtered). Users can manually define features via features/manual.json override.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Build time increases significantly for large projects
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: All new phases are incremental except feature discovery. Phases can be skipped via CLI flags (--skip-vectors, --skip-features). Embedding generation is parallelizable.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Storage growth for vector indexes in version control
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: .knowledge/vectors/ added to .gitignore by default. Vectors are regenerated from summaries (derived data, not source of truth).

success_metrics.baseline: 5-8 tool calls for 'find and modify relevant code' on 3000-file project
success_metrics.target: 2-4 tool calls (semantic_search directly surfaces relevant files)
success_metrics.measurement: Count MCP tool invocations in agent transcripts for standardized tasks

success_metrics.baseline: ~40% (substring matching returns many irrelevant results at scale)
success_metrics.measurement: Manual relevance judgment on 50 test queries across 3 projects

success_metrics.baseline: ~10% (agents fail to find all relevant files for 'how does X work' queries)
success_metrics.measurement: Evaluate get_feature_context output completeness against human-labeled feature file lists

success_metrics.target: <90 seconds total including embeddings and graph (6x overhead acceptable)
success_metrics.measurement: CI timing on standardized 3000-file TypeScript project

constraints: All new storage must remain under .knowledge/ — no external databases, no running services
System must work fully offline with local Ollama embeddings — cloud APIs are optional enhancements
Existing MCP tools must remain backward compatible — new features are additive, not breaking
No new runtime dependencies may exceed 20MB installed size (LanceDB is the largest at ~15MB)
Vector indexes are derived data and must be regenerable from summaries + symbols (not a source of truth)
Build pipeline must remain idempotent — running twice on unchanged project produces identical output
All new tools must respect the existing response-budget.ts byte limits and section priority system
Feature discovery LLM calls must use the existing summarizer-factory.ts infrastructure (Anthropic/Ollama/Claude Code backends)
acceptance_criteria: semantic_search MCP tool returns relevant results for natural language queries on a 3000-file project
explore_graph MCP tool supports multi-hop traversal across all 6 edge types with configurable depth
get_feature_context MCP tool returns cross-cutting feature summaries with involved files and data flow
find_symbol with >20 substring matches applies vector reranking and returns more precise top-10
Embedding generation runs incrementally — only changed files are re-embedded
Graph building completes in <5 seconds for 3000-file project
System works without vector index (graceful degradation to existing behavior)
System works without cloud API keys (local Ollama embeddings as default)
All existing tests continue to pass without modification
Build time for 3000-file project with all phases enabled is under 90 seconds (excluding feature discovery)
Storage overhead for vector index + graph is under 20MB for 3000-file project
